name: COI Custom Corpus
description: A small fixed set of prompts spanning the workload mix COI considers representative of agentic AI in 2026. Tokenizer-stable: each prompt is short enough that tokenizer drift between providers stays bounded. Run this corpus against every tracked model and record (input_tokens, output_tokens) per provider.

prompts.prompt: In one paragraph, explain why a company would choose to fine-tune an open-weight model rather than prompt-engineer a frontier model.

Summarize the following in 5 bullet points: The 2026 inference market is characterized by aggressive below-cost pricing from major providers, an emerging gap between sticker price and effective cost-per-task driven by tokenizer changes, and rapidly growing per-customer consumption that has outpaced enterprise budget assumptions. SemiAnalysis estimates frontier-tier output costs at $12–$28 per million tokens. Hyperscaler capex announcements for compute totaled $320B in Q1 2026 against industry token demand growth of approximately 380% year over year.

Write a Python function `effective_cost(input_tokens, output_tokens, input_rate_per_mtok, output_rate_per_mtok, tokenizer_factor=1.0)` that returns the cost in USD for a single inference call. Include a docstring and one example call in a comment.

Given the function signature `get_price(provider: str, model: str, date: str) -> dict`, produce a JSON object representing a single tool call to retrieve Anthropic Opus 4.7 pricing for 2026-05-22. Output only the JSON.

Extract the following fields from this text into a JSON object: provider, model, input_price_per_mtok, output_price_per_mtok, effective_date. Text: 'Effective April 1, 2026, Anthropic launched Opus 4.7 at $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens.' Output only valid JSON.

A frontier model charges $5/MTok input and $25/MTok output. A typical agentic workflow uses 2000 input tokens and 600 output tokens per call. If the tokenizer changes such that the same text now produces 35% more tokens, what is the new effective cost per call, in dollars to four decimal places?

The following list contains 12 items. Return only items 3, 7, and 11 as a numbered list:
1. apple
2. banana
3. cherry
4. date
5. elderberry
6. fig
7. grape
8. honeydew
9. iceberg lettuce (not a fruit, included to test for filtering)
10. jackfruit
11. kiwi
12. lemon

Rewrite the following in a neutral analytical voice, removing hype: 'AI is going to completely transform every industry by next year — companies that aren't aggressively adopting now will be left in the dust as the velocity of change accelerates exponentially.'

Classify each of these as either subsidy-positive (favors below-cost pricing continuing) or subsidy-negative (puts upward pressure on prices). One word answer per item.
- Hyperscaler announces $80B capex hike
- IPO filing for major lab
- Open-weight release matching frontier benchmarks
- New GPU generation 2x energy efficient
- Geopolitical chip export restrictions
